defectRule: ⚠️ CRITICAL — DO NOT GENERATE NEW DEFECTS: PRESERVE ALL DEFECTS EXACTLY AS-IS: Do NOT fix, hide, retouch, or improve any physical defects visible in the original photo. Keep exactly as shown: wall stains, water damage marks, peeling paint, cracked paint, cracks in walls or ceilings, chipped tiles, scuff marks, yellowing, rust stains, broken fixtures. NEVER add, invent, or introduce new stains, cracks, chips, scratches, dirt, grime, or any damage that is not already visible in the original photo. Additionally: Do NOT add any new cracking, flaking, peeling, yellowing, rust, mould, water marks, smudges, or deterioration of any kind to walls, ceilings, tiles, fixtures, or surfaces. Any surface that looks clean in the original must remain clean after editing.
fixtureRule: CRITICAL — NEVER ALTER EXISTING FIXTURES: Do NOT alter, redraw, redesign, replace, upgrade, or change the style/model/appearance of any permanent fixture or built element: sink, faucet, bathtub, shower, toilet, oven, hob, refrigerator body and door panels, kitchen cabinets, countertop surface and edge profile, doors, window frames, flooring, wall tiles, ceiling, radiators, handles, light fixtures. If removing a portable item would expose a surface behind it — fill that area with the exact continuation of the existing background surface texture only. Do NOT substitute a different fixture style or model.
qualityFooter: ABSOLUTE FINAL RULES — no exceptions: (1) Do NOT change the camera position, angle, perspective, field of view, or focal length. (2) Do NOT alter, recolor, or add any texture, pattern, or finish to walls, floors, ceiling, tiles, or any surface — all surfaces must remain exactly as in the original. (3) Do NOT add grunge, aging, film grain, vignette, artistic filters, or decorative effects. (4) Do NOT add any new object, person, animal, shadow, or design element not already present in the original. (5) Do NOT redesign the space — this is photo editing, not interior design. (6) Remove any visible reflection of the photographer, camera, or tripod from mirrors, glass, TV screens, and appliance surfaces. (7) Preserve all existing natural and artificial lighting and all shadows exactly as in the original. (8) Do NOT alter, redraw, or substitute any existing fixture — preserve the exact style, model, and condition of every permanent element. (9) Output at maximum available resolution. Do NOT downscale the image. Preserve or increase sharpness and detail.

empty.кухня:
TASK: Edit this kitchen photo to show a completely empty shell — as if ready for a new owner.

⚠️ IMPORTANT: After removing items, fill exposed wall and surface areas with the exact continuation of the existing wall texture, paint, and tile. DO NOT add, generate, or introduce any new cracks, chips, stains, water marks, or deterioration to walls, tiles, cabinet surfaces, or countertops. Surfaces must look exactly as clean (or as worn) as they already appear in the original photo — no new damage, no improvement.

REMOVE EVERY SINGLE ONE of the following items:
• ALL items on or stuck to the refrigerator surface: magnets, notes, sticky notes, drawings, children's artwork, stickers, printed photos, letters, calendars, shopping lists — the refrigerator exterior must be completely bare and clean
• All freestanding small appliances: coffee machine, kettle, toaster, blender, food processor, microwave (if not built-in)
• ALL items on every countertop: bowls, containers, bottles, jars, trays, fruit bowls, paper, boxes, plants
• All kitchen towels, dish cloths, oven mitts — anywhere they appear
• All bar stools, chairs, and seating
• All rugs, mats, anti-fatigue mats
• Curtains, blinds, and all window treatments (leave bare window frame and glass only)
• Trash cans and recycling bins
• Cleaning supplies, sprays, and brushes
• All food items, packaging, and drink containers
• All dishes, glasses, cookware, and utensils
• Any items stored on top of cabinets

KEEP ONLY these permanently built-in elements:
• Built-in kitchen cabinets and drawers
• Countertops (must be completely clear and empty)
• Built-in oven and hob integrated into cabinetry
• Panel-fitted integrated refrigerator (only if it matches the cabinetry face)
• Sink and faucet
• Fixed wall and floor tiles
• Permanently mounted ceiling and under-cabinet lighting

empty.дрон:
TASK: Enhance this aerial drone real estate photograph.

SKY: If the sky is overcast, grey, flat, or dull — replace it with a natural bright blue sky with realistic white clouds. The sky replacement must match the direction and angle of sunlight visible on the ground below. Ensure a seamless and natural blend at the horizon and roofline.

IMAGE QUALITY: Increase colour vibrancy and saturation moderately for an appealing but realistic look. Improve contrast and overall clarity. Remove atmospheric haze.

GROUND CONTENT: Keep absolutely all buildings, roads, vehicles, structures, trees, water features, and land features exactly as they are in original positions — do not move, add, or remove any ground-level element.

declutter.кухня:
TASK: Professionally declutter and clean this kitchen photo for real estate marketing. Remove personal items and clutter only — do not redesign or add anything.

⚠️ IMPORTANT: After removing items, fill exposed wall and surface areas with the exact continuation of the existing wall texture, paint, and tile. DO NOT add, generate, or introduce any new cracks, chips, stains, water marks, or deterioration to walls, tiles, cabinet surfaces, or countertops. Surfaces must look exactly as clean (or as worn) as they already appear in the original photo — no new damage, no improvement.

REMOVE EVERY SINGLE ONE of the following items:
• EVERYTHING on or attached to the refrigerator exterior: ALL magnets, stickers, notes, sticky notes, handwritten notes, drawings, children's artwork, printed photos, letters, to-do lists, calendars, shopping lists, paper clips — the entire fridge surface must be completely bare and clean
• All personal family photos and photo frames anywhere in the room
• Dirty dishes, pots, and pans in the sink or on counters
• Food packaging, open containers, and food scraps
• Kitchen towels and dish cloths draped over oven handle, sink, or counters
• Trash cans and recycling bins
• Cleaning products, sprays, and washing-up liquid
• Excess scattered utensils and cutlery on counters
• Clutter on top of cabinets and on every surface

KEEP — do not remove these:
• Built-in appliances, kitchen cabinets, countertops, sink
• A single neat fruit bowl or a small tidy plant already present and clean
• Neatly arranged built-in or countertop appliances that are part of the kitchen design

DO NOT add any new objects, decorations, or design elements not already in the photo.

evening.label: Вечер
evening.prompt:
LIGHTING TRANSFORMATION — WARM EVENING INTERIOR:
This is a time-of-day lighting conversion. Apply ALL of the following changes simultaneously:
1. CEILING & BUILT-IN LIGHTS: Every ceiling fixture, recessed spotlight, pendant light, and under-cabinet strip light must appear switched ON, emitting a warm soft yellow-orange glow (2800–3000K color temperature). Show the light cone and warm pool of light below each fixture.
2. LAMPS: Every floor lamp and table lamp visible in the scene must appear switched ON with a warm amber-yellow glow emanating from the shade.
3. WINDOWS & GLASS DOORS: Replace any daylight visible through windows with a warm dusk/evening exterior — amber-orange horizon gradient fading to dark indigo-blue upper sky. The window itself should glow with this warm exterior light spill.
4. OVERALL COLOR CAST: The entire room should take on a warm golden-amber color cast from the active interior light sources (2800K). Slightly deepen shadows in the corners away from light sources.
5. ATMOSPHERE: The result must look like a professionally photographed warm evening interior — cozy, inviting, luxury real estate feel.
Do NOT move, add, or remove any furniture, fixtures, or room objects. The room layout stays identical — only the lighting and time-of-day appearance changes.

bluehour.label: Синий час
bluehour.prompt:
LIGHTING TRANSFORMATION — BLUE HOUR / MAGIC HOUR:
This is a time-of-day lighting conversion to the iconic blue hour look. Apply ALL of the following changes simultaneously:
1. WINDOWS & GLASS DOORS: Replace any sky or exterior visible through windows and glass doors with a deep rich saturated blue twilight sky — intense royal blue to indigo gradient. This cool blue light should spill into the room from window edges, casting a soft cool blue fill light on surfaces near the windows.
2. CEILING & BUILT-IN LIGHTS: Every ceiling fixture, recessed spotlight, pendant, and under-cabinet light must appear switched ON, emitting warm 2800–3000K golden light — creating a striking warm-vs-cool contrast.
3. LAMPS: Every visible floor lamp and table lamp must appear ON with warm amber-gold glow.
4. CONTRAST: The defining characteristic of this look is the simultaneous presence of WARM interior artificial light (golden) and COOL blue exterior twilight through windows. Both must be clearly visible.
5. ATMOSPHERE: The result must look like a premium twilight real estate interior photo — dramatic, luxurious, editorial quality.
Do NOT move, add, or remove any furniture, fixtures, or room objects. The room layout stays identical — only the lighting changes.

night.label: Ночь
night.prompt:
LIGHTING TRANSFORMATION — NIGHT INTERIOR:
This is a time-of-day lighting conversion to a nighttime interior. Apply ALL of the following changes simultaneously:
1. WINDOWS & GLASS DOORS: Replace any sky or exterior visible through windows with a dark night exterior — near-black deep blue sky. Optionally show faint city ambient glow or distant exterior lighting at the very bottom of the window to suggest urban surroundings.
2. CEILING & BUILT-IN LIGHTS: Every ceiling fixture, recessed spotlight, and pendant light must appear switched ON, emitting warm white 2700–3000K light as the sole illumination source of the room.
3. LAMPS: Every visible floor lamp and table lamp must appear ON with warm glow.
4. INTERIOR MOOD: The room is entirely lit by artificial interior light only — no daylight, no twilight. Warm pools of light under each fixture, darker areas in corners and ceiling edges.
5. ATMOSPHERE: The result must look like a professionally shot nighttime interior — intimate, sophisticated, architecturally lit.
Do NOT move, add, or remove any furniture, fixtures, or room objects. The room layout stays identical — only the lighting changes.
